name: Peanut Butter-Filled Banana Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Unsalted butter at room temperature
1/3 cup Finely chopped and toasted walnuts
1/4 cup Unsalted butter at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
Cookies: Preheat oven to 350°F. Beat butter until creamy in a large bowl. Slowly beat in 3/4 cup sugar and vanilla until light and fluffy. Add banana and beat on medium speed for 1 minute. Beat in flour, salt, and walnuts. Drop batter by spoonfuls, about 2 1/2 inches apart, onto a cookie sheet sprayed lightly with nonstick cooking spray. Press the bottom of a drinking glass into sugar to press down each ball of batter to form a 2-inch circle. Bake for 15 minutes or until edges are golden brown. Remove baked cookies to a wire rack to cool.
For filling: Beat together powdered sugar, butter, cream cheese, peanut butter, vanilla extract, and nutmeg until smooth. Spread some of the filling on half the cookies. Top with remaining cookies to create sandwich cookies.
